content:
# Non-Uniqueness

**Summary**: The condition where multiple different models can fit the same set of observed data equally well.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2023-edigbue|Edigbue, 2023]] addressed non-uniqueness in joint inversion by using cross-gradient constraints and multi-objective optimization.

## Scientific Context

- [[2023-edigbue|Edigbue, 2023]] A fundamental problem in geophysics that requires regularization or joint inversion to solve.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2023-edigbue|Edigbue, 2023]] Addressed via cross-gradient constraints and multi-objective optimization.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- One of the challenges in joint inversion is dealing with the non-uniqueness of the solution. ([[2023-edigbue|Edigbue, 2023, p. 20]])
